sections.paragraph:
i.q. נוּץ-

(1) to shine, to glitter, only found Eze 1:7 whence נִיצֹוץ a spark.

(2) to flower, to flourish (as in Ch.); whence נֵץ, נִצָּה, נִצָּן a flower. (Verbs which signify shining are transferred to the meaning of verdure and bloom, as has been shewn by many examples in Simonis Arcanum Formarum, page 352; comp. זָהָה, זִו.) The idea of blossoming is farther transferred to the feathers and pinions of birds, as growing out (comp. פָּרַח); whence-

(3) to fly (compare פָּרַח to sprout, Syr. to fly); whence נֵץ hawk; comp. the kindred נָצָא, נָצָה to fly.
